title: Homer Vs The Eighteenth Amendment

jokes.text: You sure look stupid in that green dress, Lise.
jokes.setup: St. Patrick's Day scene with Lisa wearing green
jokes.punchline: Bart's blunt insult about her appearance
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart sibling antagonism, economical and true to character

jokes.text: That's funny. I don't feel stupid.
jokes.punchline: Lisa's matter-of-fact response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa response - intelligent, deadpan, subverts expected defensive reaction

jokes.text: It's been St. Patrick's Day for hours, and I'm still not drunk yet!
jokes.punchline: Barney treating sobriety as a personal failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barney line - treats drinking as an obligation, subverts normal holiday complaint

jokes.text: Where are the designated drivers? Beat it! I got no room for cheapskates.
jokes.punchline: Moe kicking out the responsible people as bad for business
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of bar economics and moral inversion, very Moe

jokes.text: everyone is a little bit Irish... except, of course, for the gays and the Italians.
jokes.punchline: Arbitrary exclusions that make no sense
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist commentary on arbitrary prejudices and fake inclusivity

jokes.text: I get bored with them. But that won't happen this time.
jokes.setup: Marge pointing out Bart always throws plastic horns away
jokes.punchline: Bart's obvious lie about not doing it again
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's transparent manipulation

jokes.text: Beep-beep! Comin' through! Hey, Notre Dame, move it or lose it.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to get through crowd
jokes.punchline: Calling someone 'Notre Dame' as insult about their size/bulk
jokes.explanation: Clever Homer insult using architectural reference for someone's bulk

jokes.text: Will the owner of the great big butt please move it out of my face.
jokes.setup: Homer continuing to be rude in crowd
jokes.punchline: Treating someone's body part like lost property announcement
jokes.explanation: Solid Homer rudeness, elevated by PA announcement format

jokes.text: All this drinking, violence, destruction of property- Are these the things we think of when we think of the Irish?
jokes.punchline: Listing exactly the stereotypical behaviors while condemning them
jokes.explanation: Smart satire of media hypocrisy - reinforcing stereotypes while condemning them

jokes.text: Hey, look, everybody! Free beer!
jokes.punchline: Homer starting the chaos by announcing free beer
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catalyst moment - his simple action triggers major consequences

jokes.text: Everybody- Everybody get naked!
jokes.punchline: Corporate mascot encouraging public nudity
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing how corporate promotion can go wildly wrong

jokes.text: Look at me! I'm the prime minister of Ireland!
jokes.punchline: Child declaring himself head of foreign government
jokes.explanation: Good drunk child logic - grandiose delusions fitting the Irish theme

jokes.text: The innocent words of a drunken child. Well, I'll tell you what we're looking at, young man. A town gone mad!
jokes.setup: Bart's slurred question 'What are you looking at?'
jokes.punchline: Kent's overwrought moral proclamation in response
jokes.explanation: Excellent contrast between innocent child question and apocalyptic media response

jokes.text: A town whose very conscience was washed away in a tide of beer and green vomit.
jokes.punchline: Poetic language culminating in 'green vomit'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant contrast of flowery prose with gross reality - perfect TV news satire

jokes.text: What about that freezer lady in Georgia?
jokes.setup: Marge calling herself world's worst mother
jokes.punchline: Homer referencing some horrible real crime for comparison
jokes.explanation: Dark Homer comfort - making Marge feel better by referencing true crime

jokes.text: They tried that in the movies, and it didn't work.
jokes.punchline: Homer confusing movies with reality as evidence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - treating fiction as historical evidence with complete confidence

jokes.text: It tastes great, makes women appear more attractive and makes a person virtually invulnerable to criticism.
jokes.punchline: Honest list of alcohol's 'benefits' in ascending order of ridiculousness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation with perfect rule of three - each benefit worse than the last

jokes.text: Won't somebody please think of the children?
jokes.punchline: Helen's signature dramatic plea
jokes.explanation: Iconic Helen Lovejoy moment - became a cultural meme beyond the show

jokes.text: All our founding fathers, astronauts and World Series heroes have been either drunk or on cocaine.
jokes.punchline: Claiming American heroes were all substance users
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum logic - completely wrong historical facts delivered with authority

jokes.text: There's also a law requiring ducks to wear long pants.
jokes.punchline: Even more ridiculous law about duck clothing
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd throwaway line that makes prohibition seem reasonable by comparison

jokes.text: Wait a minute! Let me see that! Well, I'll be darned. Long pants.
jokes.punchline: Quimby taking it seriously and being genuinely impressed
jokes.explanation: Great reaction beat - politician's amazement at bureaucratic absurdity

jokes.text: I predict our new alcohol-free Duff Zero will sell even better than our previous brand.
jokes.punchline: Executive's obvious lie about non-alcoholic beer being better
jokes.explanation: Good corporate satire about obviously false marketing claims

jokes.text: Well, that's the end of me.
jokes.punchline: Barney's matter-of-fact acceptance of his doom
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barney moment - treats loss of alcohol as existential crisis with resigned acceptance

jokes.text: That was a scary couple of hours.
jokes.punchline: Treating prohibition as brief inconvenience
jokes.explanation: Great understatement showing how quickly illegal businesses adapt

jokes.text: I don't know why people are always bad-mouthing the Mafia.
jokes.punchline: Mob boss wondering why people dislike organized crime
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - criminal's genuine confusion about negative reputation

jokes.text: [Gasps] Pervert!
jokes.setup: Wiggum trying to use charm to get out of trouble
jokes.punchline: Townsperson misinterpreting his gesture as perverted
jokes.explanation: Quick character beat showing Helen's tendency to assume the worst

jokes.text: You're just a bunch of low-income nobodies!
jokes.punchline: Politician's contempt for his constituents
jokes.explanation: Classic political satire - politician accidentally revealing true feelings about voters

jokes.text: Election in November. Election in November. What? Again? This stupid country.
jokes.setup: Quimby trying to recover from insult
jokes.punchline: His confusion and disdain for democratic process
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing corrupt politician's genuine confusion about accountability

jokes.text: How do you know you don't like bribes if you've never taken one? Here.
jokes.punchline: Mob logic about trying new experiences
jokes.explanation: Brilliant criminal logic - treating corruption like trying new food

jokes.text: From now on, we'll stick to smuggling heroin. See that you do!
jokes.punchline: Criminals switching to worse crimes with cop's approval
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark satire about prohibition's unintended consequences and law enforcement priorities

jokes.text: But without beer, prohibition doesn't work.
jokes.punchline: Completely backwards logic about prohibition's purpose
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum logic - missing the entire point of the policy he was supposed to enforce

jokes.text: Can I arrest any of you people for anything? No. Aw, geez. I don't have anything to do anymore.
jokes.punchline: Desperately asking random people if he can arrest them
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Wiggum's desperation and misunderstanding of police work

jokes.text: We're going out, Marge! If we don't come back, avenge our deaths!
jokes.setup: Homer and Bart going on simple mission
jokes.punchline: Treating beer run like dangerous military operation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's dramatic overreaction to mundane situation

jokes.text: All right!
jokes.punchline: Marge's casual acceptance
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction beat - Marge's matter-of-fact response to Homer's melodrama

jokes.text: Rex Banner. What a dope! Yeah. Look at him try to aim that stupid gun.
jokes.setup: Homer and Bart mocking Rex Banner's competence
jokes.punchline: Banner immediately shoots at them, proving his accuracy
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/punchline with perfect timing - mockery immediately disproven

jokes.text: Dad, knocking over a gravestone is bad luck. Really? I heard good.
jokes.punchline: Homer having opposite superstition with same confidence
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Homer's contrarian logic and confidence in wrong information

jokes.text: Before! Before! You're livin' in the past, Marge. Quit livin' in the past.
jokes.punchline: Homer treating 'before damage' as ancient history
jokes.explanation: Good Homer deflection - turning reasonable question into philosophical criticism

jokes.text: Going bowling. Not back, avenge deaths.
jokes.punchline: Shortened version of his previous dramatic exit
jokes.explanation: Good callback with economical repetition showing this is now routine

jokes.text: Yeah, suck like a fox!
jokes.setup: Friends saying Homer sucks at bowling
jokes.punchline: Homer's mangled version of 'crazy like a fox'
jokes.explanation: Good malapropism showing Homer's garbled attempts at clever comebacks

jokes.text: This better be the best-tastin' beer in the world. You got lucky.
jokes.punchline: Homer's non-reassuring response about quality
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Homer's honest business practices

jokes.text: Oh. So many wasted nights.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining real money is in bootlegging not vandalism
jokes.punchline: Bart regretting his amateur criminal career
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about child criminal aspirations with perfect regretful tone

jokes.text: Is some blind tiger jerking suds on the side?
jokes.setup: Banner interrogating drunk about illegal beer source
jokes.punchline: Using authentic 1920s prohibition slang seriously
jokes.explanation: Great period-accurate slang that sounds ridiculous to modern ears

jokes.text: Well, it is. I've known your father since high school and this is the cleverest thing he's ever done.
jokes.setup: Homer's illegal brewing operation being discovered
jokes.punchline: Marge's backhanded compliment about his criminal enterprise
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Marge's honest assessment of Homer's intelligence through crime

jokes.text: [All] Go to your room, Lisa!
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to make moral point about law-breaking
jokes.punchline: Entire family in unison telling her to leave
jokes.explanation: Perfect family dynamic - everyone united against Lisa's moral correctness

jokes.text: What kind of pet shop is filled with rambunctious yahoos and hot jazz music at 1:00 a.m?
jokes.setup: Moe claiming his speakeasy is a pet shop
jokes.punchline: Banner listing obvious evidence it's not a pet shop
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the punchline, Banner being logical about obvious lie

jokes.text: But you people remember, baby turtles and alligators may seem like a cute idea for a pet but they grow up.
jokes.setup: Banner accepting the pet shop story
jokes.punchline: Giving actual pet advice at a speakeasy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment - earnest public service announcement in criminal context

jokes.text: You're out there somewhere, Beer Baron and I'll find you. No, you won't. Yes, I will. Won't.
jokes.setup: Banner's dramatic vow to catch the Beer Baron
jokes.punchline: Homer responding like a child from hiding
jokes.explanation: Good comedy deflating Banner's dramatic moment with childish response

jokes.text: Look at me, Rex Banner. I have a new hat.
jokes.setup: Documentary narrator describing Beer Baron's arrogance
jokes.punchline: Treating a hat as a major provocation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant documentary parody - turning mundane detail into dramatic criminal taunt

jokes.text: Call me Beer Baron.
jokes.punchline: Homer insisting on his criminal alias with his son
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's pride in his criminal identity

jokes.text: But I can't be out of beer. I'm the Beer Baron!
jokes.setup: Bart explaining they're out of beer supplies
jokes.punchline: Homer treating his title as magical beer-creating power
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic confusion between title and actual capabilities

jokes.text: How about some turpentine? Or caulk? Delicious caulk?
jokes.punchline: Homer offering increasingly toxic alternatives enthusiastically
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation with Homer's enthusiastic sales pitch for poison

jokes.text: You know, they're 50 for $3,000 today.
jokes.punchline: Salesman pushing bulk discount for obvious illegal activity
jokes.explanation: Good business satire about willful ignorance of criminal customers

jokes.text: We're patriots, like all those people in jail.
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to prisoners as patriots
jokes.explanation: Excellent satirical logic about criminal self-justification

jokes.text: Well, if you're talking about root beer I plead guilt-diddily-ilty as char-diddily-arged.
jokes.setup: Rex Banner asking if Ned is the Beer Baron
jokes.punchline: Flanders' over-the-top confession to legal activity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Flanders moment with characteristic speech pattern and innocent guilt

jokes.text: Yes, but only by night. By day, I'm a mild-mannered reporter for a major metropolitan newspaper.
jokes.setup: Banner asking if he's the Beer Baron
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy using Superman's secret identity
jokes.explanation: Good meta-reference perfectly suited to Comic Book Guy's character

jokes.text: What, dear? Kablamo!
jokes.punchline: Homer making sound effects to cover actual explosion
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with Homer's childish attempt at deception

jokes.text: Yeah, I had to sell the trigger and most of the handle to feed my family.
jokes.setup: Homer pointing out Wiggum's gun has no trigger
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's desperate explanation of poverty
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy about economic desperation with absurd specificity

jokes.text: Come on! Give me the dough! I can throw this pretty hard!
jokes.punchline: Threatening to throw the useless weapon
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Wiggum's desperation and incompetence

jokes.text: The idea that someone like that could operate under my very nose is laughable.
jokes.setup: Banner claiming Beer Baron doesn't exist
jokes.punchline: His confident dismissal while Homer operates nearby
jokes.explanation: Classic dramatic irony setup for Banner's comeuppance

jokes.text: Well, you all know what laughter sounds like.
jokes.punchline: Awkward clarification when nobody laughs
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment when confident character's joke falls flat

jokes.text: I filled the balls with a funnel.
jokes.setup: Wiggum explaining Homer's elaborate bowling ball system
jokes.punchline: Homer's simple correction about the obvious detail
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's attention to obvious practical details

jokes.text: Sorry. I thought you'd get a fine... or at most, three, four years in jail.
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's casual attitude about sending friend to prison
jokes.explanation: Wiggum's characteristic obliviousness to consequences

jokes.text: Sorry. I thought you'd get a fine or at most, three, four years in jail.
jokes.setup: Homer being sentenced to catapult execution
jokes.punchline: His casual expectation of moderate punishment for serious crime
jokes.explanation: Good Homer obliviousness about consequences of his actions

jokes.text: If it were, I'd kill everyone who looked at me cockeyed!
jokes.setup: Banner saying we can't choose which laws to obey
jokes.punchline: His violent hypothetical about personal law enforcement
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy revealing Banner's authoritarian mindset through extreme example

jokes.text: Well, sorry. I'm not in that business anymore. Four minutes.
jokes.setup: Town asking how long to get beer flowing again
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate contradiction of his retirement
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and character moment showing Homer can't resist opportunity

jokes.text: To alcohol, the cause of and solution to all of life's problems.
jokes.setup: Homer making a toast at episode's end
jokes.punchline: Philosophical contradiction about alcohol's role
jokes.explanation: Iconic Homer toast that became one of the most quoted Simpsons lines - perfect philosophical contradiction

tmdb_overview: When Prohibition is revived in Springfield, Homer and Bart supply the town with alcohol.
insight: Prohibition parody sustains 81-point humor through relentless character absurdism and irony.
